THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter and Weston moved forward on infrastructure and development decisions this week, while Marathon County learned it was approved for FEMA individual assistance — but denied public assistance — following the April 2026 storms.

  • Marathon County was approved for FEMA individual assistance after April storms but denied public assistance due to insufficient documented damages; 24 county residents have already applied at disasterassistance.gov.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board approved an amended bid of $814,815 for a six-bay equipment garage, adding a second restroom, and approved ballot drop box placement for the August primary and November general elections.
  • Weston's Public Works Committee awarded the Concord Avenue and Bayberry Street reconstruction bid to HS Suns, including a sidewalk alternate, for roughly $2,248,819.90, and awarded a $12,500 surveying contract for Howland Avenue.
  • Weston's Plan Commission unanimously approved a conditional use permit for a 1,090-square-foot detached accessory dwelling unit at 4804 Shore Avenue, prohibiting short-term rental use, and forwarded approval of the 23-lot Bull Junior View final plat to the village board.
  • Kronenwetter's Plan Commission is set to hold public hearings Monday on a zoning change for Marathon County Highway Department property — from B3, SF and AR to M2 General Industrial — and a conditional use permit for a residential pond.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate, including Weston's Board of Trustees at 6:00 p.m. and Kronenwetter's revised Plan Commission at 6:00 p.m., where zoning and development requests are on the table; Wausau's Plan Commission meets Tuesday at 5:00 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
